mission.title: Install and Configure Monocle on Kubernetes
mission.description: Monocle is a framework designed for tracing GenAI app code, allowing developers to easily instrument their applications for observability. Installing Monocle enables you to monitor and trace your GenAI applications in production environments seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Monocle Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Monocle Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add monocle https://monocle2ai.github.io/monocle
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Monocle using Helm
steps.description:
Install Monocle in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the desired namespace.
```bash
helm install monocle monocle/monocle --namespace monocle --create-namespace --version v0.7.6
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Monocle pods are running successfully in the specified namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monocle
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Monocle deployment to set resource limits for better performance and stability.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment monocle --namespace monocle
# Add the following under spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   requests:
#     cpu: "250m"
#     memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring
steps.description:
Integrate Monocle with your observability stack by configuring the necessary endpoints in your application code.
```bash
# In your application code, import and set up Monocle:
from monocle_apptrace.instrumentor import setup_monocle_telemetry
setup_monocle_telemetry()
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Monocle will have the Monocle pods running in the 'monocle' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and set up monitoring in your application code.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add monocle https://monocle2ai.github.io/monocle
helm repo update
helm install monocle monocle/monocle --namespace monocle --create-namespace --version v0.7.6

kubectl edit deployment monocle --namespace monocle
# Add the following under spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
#   requests:
#     cpu: "250m"
#     memory: "256Mi"

# In your application code, import and set up Monocle:
from monocle_apptrace.instrumentor import setup_monocle_telemetry
setup_monocle_telemetry()

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Monocle Helm release from your Kubernetes cluster to remove the deployment and associated resources.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the Monocle namespace and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Monocle installation to ensure you can roll back if necessary.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Monocle Helm repository and run the upgrade command to install the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check the status of the Monocle pods to ensure they are running the upgraded version successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This indicates that the Monocle pods are failing to start. Check the logs for errors using the following command: 
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace monocle
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Monocle service is not reachable, verify the service configuration and check for any network policies that might be blocking access. Use: 
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace monocle
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set for the Monocle deployment. Use: 
```bash
kubectl describe deployment monocle --namespace monocle
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check for any version compatibility issues or existing resources that may conflict. Use: 
```bash
helm history monocle --namespace monocle
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy Monocle.
